findings.description: Supabase Auth bypass via crafted OIDC tokens (Apple/Azure providers). Fixed in hosted Supabase >= v2.185.0. LOW RISK for this project: trocalia uses email/phone auth only.
findings.remediation: Verify project does not enable Apple/Azure OIDC. Already patched on hosted Supabase.

findings.description: Architectural risk: Supabase MCP uses service_role access (full DB privileges). If Claude processes untrusted user input through MCP tools, prompt injection could trigger unauthorized SQL. Documented incident: Supabase Cursor agent data leak (mid-2025).
findings.remediation: Never pass raw user-submitted content as context when invoking Supabase MCP tools. Validate and sanitize before MCP calls.

known_threats.description: RCE via PreToolUse hook execution before trust dialogs. Malicious hooks in project-level config execute automatically.
known_threats.remediation: Patched in Claude Code >= 1.0.111. Keep Claude Code updated.

known_threats.description: API key exfiltration via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL override in project config.
Auth bypass via crafted Apple/Azure OIDC ID tokens. Fixed in supabase/auth >= 2.185.0.
known_threats.remediation: Hosted Supabase patched. Self-hosted: upgrade to >= 2.185.0.

community_alerts.summary: Lethal Trifecta: Supabase MCP + service_role + untrusted input = data leak. Real incident documented.
